pattern: Addition can be rebuilt from bit logic: XOR gives the sum without carries, AND shifted left gives the carries, and repeating until no carry remains yields the total.
hints: Think about how a half-adder works: for a single bit, the sum is one exclusive-or and the carry is one and.
The exclusive-or of the two numbers adds every bit while ignoring carries, and the and of them shifted left one place is exactly where the carries land.

Loop: let the carry be the and of the two values shifted left, replace the first value with their exclusive-or, and continue until the carry is zero; mask to 32 bits and sign-extend so negatives behave.

edge_inputs.description: positive plus negative cancels to zero
negative magnitude exceeds positive so result is negative
adding zero leaves the value unchanged
carry ripples across several bit positions

stress.description: random pair across the full signed range including sign mixes
stress.note: covers all four sign combinations and carry chains
